DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert P. Travis of the law offices of Travis & Travis (415-923-1200) appears as counsel for defendants/cross-complainants/moving parties

No other appearance is made in this matter

Counsel present submits without argument. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS COMPLAINANT 301 ROLPH STREET CORPORATION, DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in part and SUSTAINED in part.

These demurrers highlight the lamentable practice of laundry-listing affirmative defenses in an answer. As cross-defendant Rajeev Kumar concedes, affirmative defenses in an answer must plead facts "as carefully and with as much detail" as a complaint does. (See FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) I thus rule on cross-complainant 301 Rolph Street Corporation's demurrers to the numbered affirmative defenses as follows:

3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 12, 22, 23: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND
14: OVERRULED.
25: This is not a valid defense, so SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.
20, 21: duplicate 14, so SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.

Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.com (L&M302)

MINI MINUTES:
